task_completion.description: Agent logged into OpenClinica using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenClinica dashboard or interface indicating a successful login (e.g., logged in as 'root').

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the study management or study list page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a list of studies or the 'Administer Studies' page in OpenClinica.

task_completion.description: Agent soft-deleted or removed the 'Cardiovascular Outcomes Registry' (CV-REG-2023) study.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Cardiovascular Outcomes Registry' study has a status of 'Removed' (or equivalent icon), or a confirmation message indicating it was successfully removed/soft-deleted.

task_completion.description: The 'Phase II Diabetes Trial' (DM-TRIAL-2024) study was not modified or removed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Phase II Diabetes Trial' study is still present in the study list with its original 'Available' status.

task_completion.description: The 'Asthma Prevention Pilot' (AP-PILOT-2022) study was not modified or removed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Asthma Prevention Pilot' study is still present in the study list with its original 'Completed' status.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenClinica web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser with the OpenClinica application open, rather than terminal windows executing database queries or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent used the application's built-in functionality to remove the study.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking the remove/delete icon or confirming the removal via the OpenClinica UI, rather than editing the page source.

integrity.description: Agent did not alter other system settings or studies.
integrity.visual_evidence: The study list and system interface appear normal with no signs of unintended changes or deletions other than the target study.
